amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: The tricarboxylic acid (TCA) cycle in E. coli is the central oxidative pathway that completes the breakdown of carbon sources to CO2 while generating reducing equivalents (NADH, FADH2) for the electron transport chain and biosynthetic precursors for amino acids and other molecules. The cycle begins with condensation of acetyl-CoA with oxaloacetate to form citrate, proceeds through eight enzyme-catalyzed steps, and regenerates oxaloacetate. E. coli's TCA cycle has unique features including branching at α-ketoglutarate (oxidative vs biosynthetic routes), use of both NADH and NADPH-generating enzymes (Icd isoforms), and reversal capability under anaerobic conditions with alternative electron acceptors. The cycle is tightly regulated by substrate availability, energy charge (NADH/NAD+), and catabolite repression. This flowchart illustrates the complete cycle, branch points, regulatory mechanisms, and biosynthetic outputs.
mermaid:
graph TD
    Start([TCA Cycle]) --> A1[Acetyl-CoA enters cycle]
    A1 --> A2["Acetyl-CoA from: glycolysis, fatty acids, amino acids"]
    
    A2 --> A3["Step 1: Citrate Synthase GltA"]
    A3 --> A4[Acetyl-CoA + Oxaloacetate + H2O]
    A4 --> A5[Citrate + CoA-SH]
    A5 --> A6[ΔG°' = -31.4 kJ/mol - irreversible]
    
    A6 --> A7["Step 2: Aconitase AcnA AcnB"]
    A7 --> A8{Which aconitase isoform?}
    A8 -->|AcnA - aerobic| A9[Iron-sulfur cluster intact]
    A8 -->|AcnB - stationary phase| A10[Alternative isoform]
    
    A9 --> A11[Citrate ⇌ cis-Aconitate + H2O]
    A10 --> A11
    A11 --> A12[cis-Aconitate ⇌ Isocitrate]
    A12 --> A13[Isocitrate formed]
    
    A13 --> A14["Step 3: Isocitrate Dehydrogenase"]
    A14 --> A15{Which Icd isoform?}
    A15 -->|Icd NADP+ dependent| A16[Produces NADPH for biosynthesis]
    A15 -->|IcdA NAD+ dependent| A17[Produces NADH for energy]
    
    A16 --> A18[Isocitrate + NADP+ → α-Ketoglutarate + CO2 + NADPH]
    A17 --> A19[Isocitrate + NAD+ → α-Ketoglutarate + CO2 + NADH]
    
    A18 --> A20[α-Ketoglutarate formed]
    A19 --> A20
    A20 --> A21[First CO2 released, first NADH/NADPH]
    
    A20 --> A22{Branch point at α-ketoglutarate}
    A22 -->|Biosynthetic| A23[α-Ketoglutarate → Glutamate]
    A22 -->|Oxidative - continue cycle| A24["Step 4: α-Ketoglutarate Dehydrogenase Complex"]
    
    A23 --> A25[Biosynthetic withdrawal for amino acids]
    
    A24 --> A26[α-Ketoglutarate Dehydrogenase KDH complex]
    A26 --> A27["E1: SucA - decarboxylase"]
    A27 --> A28["E2: SucB - dihydrolipoyl transsuccinylase"]
    A28 --> A29["E3: LpdA - dihydrolipoyl dehydrogenase shared with PDH"]
    
    A29 --> A30{Substrates available?}
    A30 -->|α-KG + NAD+ + CoA| A31[Complex reaction proceeds]
    A30 -->|Missing substrate| Wait1[Wait for substrates]
    Wait1 --> A30
    
    A31 --> A32[α-Ketoglutarate + NAD+ + CoA-SH → Succinyl-CoA + CO2 + NADH]
    A32 --> A33[Second CO2 released, second NADH]
    A33 --> A34[Succinyl-CoA formed - high energy thioester]
    
    A34 --> A35{Succinyl-CoA fate}
    A35 -->|Biosynthetic| A36[Porphyrin synthesis]
    A35 -->|Oxidative - continue| A37["Step 5: Succinyl-CoA Synthetase SucCD"]
    
    A36 --> A38[Biosynthetic withdrawal for heme]
    
    A37 --> A39[Succinyl-CoA + GDP + Pi → Succinate + CoA + GTP]
    A39 --> A40[Substrate-level phosphorylation - only ATP equivalent]
    A40 --> A41[GTP formed can phosphorylate ADP]
    
    A41 --> A42["Step 6: Succinate Dehydrogenase SdhABCD"]
    A42 --> A43[Membrane-bound complex II]
    A43 --> A44[Contains FAD, Fe-S clusters, heme b]
    
    A44 --> A45[Succinate + FAD → Fumarate + FADH2]
    A45 --> A46[FADH2 directly feeds electrons to quinone pool]
    A46 --> A47[Part of electron transport chain]
    
    A47 --> A48{Oxygen availability?}
    A48 -->|Aerobic| A49[Electrons to O2 via ETC]
    A48 -->|Anaerobic| A50[Reverse to fumarate reductase FrdABCD]
    
    A50 --> A51[Fumarate → Succinate using NADH]
    A51 --> A52[Anaerobic respiration with fumarate as acceptor]
    
    A49 --> A53["Step 7: Fumarase FumABC"]
    A53 --> A54{Which fumarase?}
    A54 -->|FumA - aerobic| A55[Class I fumarase]
    A54 -->|FumB - all conditions| A56[Class I fumarase]
    A54 -->|FumC - aerobic Fe-S resistant| A57[Class II fumarase]
    
    A55 --> A58[Fumarate + H2O ⇌ L-Malate]
    A56 --> A58
    A57 --> A58
    
    A58 --> A59[L-Malate formed]
    
    A59 --> A60["Step 8: Malate Dehydrogenase Mdh"]
    A60 --> A61[L-Malate + NAD+ ⇌ Oxaloacetate + NADH + H+]
    A61 --> A62[Third NADH produced]
    
    A62 --> A63[Oxaloacetate regenerated]
    A63 --> A64[Cycle complete - ready for next acetyl-CoA]
    
    A64 --> A65{Continue cycling?}
    A65 -->|Yes - acetyl-CoA available| A3
    A65 -->|No| End1([Cycle paused])
    
    A21 --> A66[Reducing equivalents summary per cycle]
    A33 --> A66
    A40 --> A66
    A46 --> A66
    A62 --> A66
    
    A66 --> A67[3 NADH produced]
    A67 --> A68[1 FADH2 produced]
    A68 --> A69[1 GTP produced]
    A69 --> A70[2 CO2 released]
    
    A70 --> A71["Total: 3 NADH + 1 FADH2 + 1 GTP = ~10 ATP from ETC"]
    
    A71 --> End2([Energy and biosynthetic output])
    
    A25 --> A72[Biosynthetic outputs]
    A38 --> A72
    A72 --> A73[α-KG → Glu → Pro, Arg]
    A73 --> A74[Succinyl-CoA → Heme]
    A74 --> A75[Oxaloacetate → Asp → Asn, Met, Thr, Ile, Lys]
    
    A75 --> End3([Biosynthetic precursors])
    
    A52 --> A76[Anaerobic TCA cycle modifications]
    A76 --> A77[Glyoxylate shunt bypasses CO2 steps]
    A77 --> End4([Alternative modes])

name: Tricarboxylic Acid Cycle (TCA/Krebs Cycle)
notes: The TCA cycle is the central hub of aerobic metabolism, integrating energy production with biosynthesis. E. coli's cycle has unique features: isoenzymes for different conditions (AcnA vs AcnB, FumA/B/C), NADP+-dependent Icd for NADPH production, and reversibility under anaerobic conditions (fumarate reductase). Branch points at α-ketoglutarate and succinyl-CoA provide biosynthetic precursors. The cycle is regulated by substrate availability, energy charge (NADH/NAD+ ratio), and catabolite repression (ArcAB, Cra). Under anaerobiosis or on acetate, the glyoxylate shunt bypasses the CO2-producing steps. This metabolic flexibility allows E. coli to thrive in diverse environments.
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on extensive biochemical characterization of E. coli TCA cycle enzymes and regulation. All enzymes, intermediates, cofactors, and regulatory mechanisms have been validated through decades of research. The model represents how bacteria integrate energy production with biosynthesis through this central metabolic hub.
